author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
On the words of the Gospel, John v. 19, ‘The Son can do nothing of Himself, but what He seeth the Father doing.’

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel, John v. 19, ‘The Son can do nothing of Himself, but what He seeth the Father doing.’

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The mysteries and secrets of the kingdom of God first seek for believing men, that they may make them understanding.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For faith is understanding’s step; and understanding faith’s attainment.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This the Prophet expressly says to all who prematurely and in undue order look for understanding, and neglect faith.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For he says, “Unless ye believe, ye shall not understand.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Faith itself then also hath a certain light of its own in the Scriptures, in Prophecy, in the Gospel, in the Lessons of the Apostles.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For all these things which are read to us in this present time, are lights in a dark place, that we may be nourished up unto the day.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Apostle Peter says, “We have a more sure word of prophecy, whereunto ye do well that ye take heed, as unto a light in a dark place, until the day dawn, and the day-star arise in your hearts.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye see then, Brethren, how exceedingly unregulated and disordered in their haste are they who like immature conceptions seek an untimely birth before the birth; who say to us, “Why dost thou bid me believe what I do not see?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let me see something that I may believe.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou biddest me believe whilst yet I see not; I wish to see, and by seeing to believe, not by hearing.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let the Prophet speak.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Unless ye believe, ye shall not understand.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou wishest to ascend, and dost forget the steps.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Surely, out of all order.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: O man, if I could show thee already what thou mightest see, I should not exhort thee to believe.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Faith then, as it has been elsewhere defined, is “the firm support of those who hope, the evidence of things which are not seen.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If they are not seen, how are they evidenced to be?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whence are these things which thou seest, but from That which thou seest not?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To be sure thou dost see somewhat that thou mayest believe somewhat, and from that thou seest, mayest believe what thou seest not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Be not ungrateful to Him who hath made thee see, whereby thou mayest be able to believe what as yet thou canst not see.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: God hath given thee eyes in the body, reason in the heart; arouse the reason of the heart, wake up the interior inhabitant of thine interior eyes, let it take to its windows, examine the creature of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For there is one within who sees by the eyes.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For when thy thoughts within thee are on any other subject, and the inhabitant within is turned away, the things which are before thine eyes thou seest not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For to no purpose are the windows open, when he who looks through them is away.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is not then the eyes that see, but some one sees by the eyes; awake him, arouse him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For this hath not been denied thee; God hath made thee a rational animal, set thee over the cattle, formed thee after His Own image.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Oughtest thou to use them as the cattle do; only to see what to add to thy belly, not to thy soul?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Stir up, I say, the eye of reason, use thine eyes as a man should, consider the heaven and earth, the ornaments of the heaven, the fruitfulness of the earth, the flight of the birds, the swimming of the fish, the virtue of the seeds, the order of the seasons; consider the works, and seek for the Author; take a view of what thou seest, and seek Him whom thou seest not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Believe on Him whom thou seest not, because of these things which thou seest.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And lest thou think that it is with mine own words that I have exhorted thee; hear the Apostle saying, “For the invisible things of God from the creation of the world are clearly seen by those things which are made.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: These things thou disregardedst, nor didst look upon them as a man, but as an irrational animal.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Prophet cried out to thee, and cried in vain.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Be ye not like to horse and mule, which have no understanding.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: These things I say thou didst see, and disregard.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: God’s daily miracles were disesteemed, not for their easiness, but their constant repetition.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what is more difficult to understand than a man’s birth, that one who was in existence should by dying depart into darkness, and that one who was not, by being born should come forth to light?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What so marvellous, what so difficult to comprehend?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But with God easy to be done.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Marvel at these things, awake; at His unusual works, thou canst wonder, are they greater than those which thou art accustomed to see?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Men wondered that our Lord God Jesus Christ filled so many thousands with five loaves; and they do not wonder that through a few grains the whole earth is filled with crops.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When the water was made wine, men saw it, and were amazed; what else takes place with the rain along the root of the vine?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He did the one, He does the other; the one that thou mayest be fed, the other that thou mayest wonder.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But both are wonderful, for both are the works of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Man sees unusual things, and wonders; whence is the man himself who wonders? where was he? whence came he forth? whence the fashion of his body? whence the distinction of his limbs? whence that beautiful form? from what beginnings? what contemptible beginnings?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And he wonders at other things, when he the wonderer is himself a great wonder.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whence then are these things which thou seest but from Him whom thou seest not?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But as I had begun to say, because these things were disesteemed by thee, He came Himself to do unusual things, that in these usual ones too thou mightest acknowledge thy Creator.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He came to Whom it is said, “Renew signs.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To Whom it is said, “Show forth Thy marvellous mercies.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For dispensing them He ever was; He dispensed them, and no one marvelled.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore came He a Little one to the little, He came a Physician to the sick, who was able to come when He would, to return when He would, to do whatsoever He would, to judge as He would.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And this, His will, is very righteousness; yea what He willeth, I say, is very righteousness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For that is not unrighteous which He willeth, nor can that be right which He willeth not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He came to raise the dead, men marvelling that He restored a man to the light who was in light already, He who day by day bringeth forth to the light those who were not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: These things He did, yet was He despised by the many, who considered not so much what great things He did, as how small He was; as though they said within themselves, “These are divine things, but He is a man.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Two things then thou seest, divine works, and a man.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If divine works cannot be wrought but by God, take heed lest in This Man God lie concealed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Attend, I say, to what thou seest, believe what thou seest not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He hath not abandoned thee, who hath called thee to believe; though He enjoin thee to believe that which thou canst not see: yet hath He not given thee up to see nothing whereby thou mayest be able to believe what thou dost not see.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Is the creation itself a small sign, a small indication of the Creator?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He also came, He did miracles.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou couldest not see God, a man thou couldest; so God was made Man, that in One thou mightest have both what to see, and what to believe.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thus thou hearest, and as yet seest not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, He comes, lo, He is born, lo, He comes forth of a woman, who made man and woman.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He who made man and woman was not made by man and woman.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For thou wouldest peradventure have been likely to despise Him for being born, the manner of His birth canst thou not despise; for He ever was before that He was born.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, I say, He took a Body, He was clothed in Flesh, He came forth from the womb.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Dost thou now see? seest thou now, I say?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I ask as to the Flesh, but I point out as to That Flesh; something thou seest, and something thou seest not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, in this very Birth, there are at once two things, one which thou mayest see, and another thou mayest not see; but so that by this which thou seest, thou mayest believe that which thou seest not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou hadst begun to despise, because thou seest Him who was born; believe what thou dost not see, that He was born of a virgin.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “How trifling a person,” says one, “is he who was born!”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But how great is He who was of a virgin born!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And He who was born of a virgin brought thee a temporal miracle; He was not born of a father, of any man, I mean, His father, yet was He born of the flesh.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But let it not seem impossible to thee, that He was born by His mother only, Who made man before father and mother.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He brought thee then a temporal miracle, that thou mayest seek and admire Him who is Eternal.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He “who came forth as a Bridegroom out of His chamber,” that is, out of the virgin’s womb, where the holy nuptials were celebrated of the Word and the Flesh: He brought, I say, a temporal miracle; but He is Himself eternal, He is coeternal with the Father, He it is, who “In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He did for thee whereby thou mightest be cured, that thou mightest be able to see what thou didst not see.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What thou despisest in Christ, is not yet the contemplation of him that is made whole, but the medicine of the sick.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do not hasten to the vision of the whole.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Angels see, the Angels rejoice, the Angels feed Thereon and live; Whereon they feed faileth not, nor is their food minished.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In the thrones of glory, in the regions of the heavens, in the parts which are above the heavens, the Word is seen by the Angels, and is their Joy; is their Food, and endureth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But in order that man might eat Angel’s Bread, the Lord of Angels became Man.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This is our Salvation, the Medicine of the infirm, the Food of the whole.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And He spake to men, and said what ye have now heard, “The Son can do nothing of Himself, but what He seeth the Father do.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Is there now any one, think we, that understandeth this?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Is there any one, think we, in whom the eye-salve of the flesh hath now its effect to the discerning in any fashion the brightness of the Divinity?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He hath spoken, let us speak too; He, because the Word; we, because of the Word.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And why speak we, howsoever we do it, of the Word?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because we were made by the Word after the likeness of the Word.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As far then as we are capable of, as far as we can be partakers of that ineffableness, let us also speak, and let us not be contradicted.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For our faith hath gone before, so that we may say, “I believed, therefore have I spoken.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I speak then that which I believe; whether or no I also see, or howsoever I see; He seeth rather; ye cannot see it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But when I shall have spoken, whether he who sees what I speak of, believe that I see too what I have spoken of, or whether he believe it not, what is that to me?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let him only really see, and let him believe what he will of me.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “The Son can do nothing of Himself, but what He seeth the Father do.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Here rises up an error of the Arians; but it rises up that it may fall; because it is not humbled, that it may rise.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is it which hath set thee off?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou wouldest say that the Son is less than the Father.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For thou hast heard, “The Son can do nothing of Himself, but what He seeth the Father do.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: From this thou wouldest have the Son called less; it is this I know, I know it is this hath set thee off; believe that He is not less, thou canst not as yet see it, believe, this is what I was saying a little while ago.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “But how,” you will say, “am I to believe against His own words”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He saith Himself, “The Son can do nothing of Himself, but what He seeth the Father do.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Attend too to that which follows; “For what things soever the Father doeth, the same also doeth the Son likewise;” He did not say, “such things,” Beloved, consider a while, that ye cause not confusion to yourselves.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There is need of a tranquil heart, a godly and devout faith, a religious earnest attention; attend, not to me the poor vessel, but to Him who putteth the bread in the vessel.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Attend then a while.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For in all that I have said above in exhorting you to faith, that the mind imbued with faith may be capable of understanding, all that has been said has had a pleasing, glad, and easy sound, has cheered your minds, ye have followed it, ye have understood what I said.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But what I am now about to say I hope there are some who will understand; yet I fear that all will not understand.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And seeing that God hath by the lesson of the Gospel proposed to us a subject to speak upon, and we cannot avoid that which the Master hath proposed; I fear lest haply they who will not understand, who perhaps will be the greater number, should think that I have spoken to them in vain; but yet because of those who will understand, I do not speak in vain.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let him who understandeth rejoice, let him who doth not understand bear it patiently; what he doth not understand, let him bear, and that he may understand, let him bear delay.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He doth not say then, “What things soever the Father doeth, such doeth the Son:” as if the Father doeth some things, and the Son others.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For it did seem as though He had meant this when He said above, “The Son doeth nothing of Himself, but what He seeth the Father do.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Mark; He did not there either say, “But what He heareth the Father enjoin;” but, “what He seeth the Father do.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If then we consult the carnal understanding, or sense rather, He hath set before Him as it were two workmen, the Father and the Son, the Father working without seeing any, the Son working from seeing the Father.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This is still a carnal view.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Nevertheless, in order to understand those things which are higher, let us not decline these lower and mean things.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: First, let us set something before our eyes in this way; let us suppose there are two workmen, father and son.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The father has made a chest, which the son could not make, unless he saw the father making it: he keeps his mind on the chest which the father has made, and makes another chest like it, not the same.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I put off for a while the words which follow, and now I ask the Arian; “Dost thou understand it in the sense of this supposition?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hath the Father done something, which when the Son saw Him do, He too hath done something like it?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For do the words by which thou art perplexed seem to have this meaning?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now He doth not say, “The Son can do nothing of Himself, but what He heareth the Father enjoin.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But He saith, “The Son can do nothing of Himself, but what He seeth the Father do.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See, if thou understand it thus; the Father hath done something, and the Son attendeth that He may see what He Himself too hath to do; and that, some other thing like that which the Father had done.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This which the Father hath done, by whom hath He done it?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If not by the Son, if not by the Word, thou hast incurred the charge of blasphemy against the Gospel.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “For all things were made by Him.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then what the Father had done, He had done by the Word; if by the Word He had done it, He had done it by the Son.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who then is that other who attends, that He may do some other thing which he seeth the Father do?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye have not been wont to say that the Father hath two sons: there is One, One Only-Begotten of Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But through His mercy, Alone as regards His Divinity and not Alone as regards the inheritance.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Father hath made coheirs with His Only Son; not begotten them like Him of His Own Substance, but adopted them by Him out of His Own family.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For “we have been called,” as Holy Scripture testifieth, “into the adoption of sons.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then sayest thou?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is the Only Son Himself That speaketh; the Only-Begotten Son speaketh in the Gospel: the Word Himself hath given us the words, we have heard Himself saying, “The Son can do nothing of Himself, but what He seeth the Father do.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now then the Father doeth that the Son may see what to do; and nevertheless the Father doeth nothing but by the Son.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Assuredly thou art confused, thou heretic, assuredly thou art confused; but thy confusion is as from taking hellebore, that thou mayest be cured.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Even now thou canst not find thine own self, thou dost even thyself condemn thine own judgment and thy carnal view, I think.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Put behind thee the eyes of the flesh, raise up what eyes thou hast in thine heart, behold things divine.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They are men’s words it is true thou hearest, and by a man, by the Evangelist, by the Gospel thou hearest men’s words, as a man; but it is of the Word of God thou hearest, that thou mayest hear what is human, come to know what is Divine.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Master hath given trouble, that He might instruct; hath sown a difficulty, that He might excite an earnest attention.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “The Son can do nothing of Himself, but what He seeth the Father do.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It might follow that He should say, “For what things soever the Father doeth, the like doeth the Son.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This He doth not say; but, “What things soever the Father doeth, the same doeth the Son likewise.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Father doeth not some things, the Son other things; because all things that the Father doeth, He doeth by the Son.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Son raised Lazarus; did not the Father raise him?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Son gave sight to the blind man; did not the Father give him sight?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Father by the Son in the Holy Ghost.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is the Trinity; but the Operation of the Trinity is One, the Majesty One, the Eternity One, the Coeternity One, and the Works the Same.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Father doth not create some men, the Son others, the Holy Ghost others; the Father and the Son and the Holy Ghost create one and the same man; and the Father and the Son and the Holy Ghost, One God, createth him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: You observe a Plurality of Persons, but acknowledge the Unity of the Divinity.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For because of the Plurality of Persons it was said, “Let Us make man after Our image and likeness.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He did not say, “I will make man, and do Thou attend when I am making him, that Thou too mayest be able to make another.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Let Us make,” He saith; I hear the Plurality; “after Our image;” again I hear the Plurality.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Where then is the Singularity of the Divinity?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Read what follows, “And God made man.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is said, “Let Us make man;” and it is not said, “The Gods made man.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Unity is understood in that it was said, “God made man.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Where then is that carnal view?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Be it confounded, hidden, brought to nought; let the Word of God speak to us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Even now as godly men, as believing already, as already imbued with faith, and having gotten some attainment of understanding, turn we to the Word Himself, to the Fountain of light, and let us say together, “O Lord, the Father doeth ever the same things as Thou; for that whatsoever the Father doeth, by Thee He doeth it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We have heard that Thou art the Word in the beginning; we have not seen, but believed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There too have we heard what follows, that ‘all things were made by Thee.’

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: All things then that the Father doeth, He doeth by Thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore Thou doest the same things as the Father.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why then didst Thou wish to say, ‘The Son can do nothing of Himself’?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For I see a certain equality in Thee with the Father, in that I hear, ‘What things soever the Father doeth, the same doeth the Son;’ I recognise an equality, hereby I understand, and comprehend as far as I am able, ‘I and My Father are One.’

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What meaneth it, that Thou canst do nothing, but what Thou seest the Father do?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What meaneth this?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Peradventure He would say to me, yea say to us all: “Now as to this that I have said, ‘The Son can do nothing, but what He seeth the Father do;’ My ‘Seeing’ how dost thou understand?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: My ‘Seeing,’ what is it?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Put aside for a while the form of the servant which He took for thy sake.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For in that servant’s form our Lord had eyes and ears in the Flesh, and that human form was the same figure of a Body, such as we bear, the same outlines of members.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That Flesh had come from Adam: but He was not as Adam.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then the Lord walking whether on the earth or in the sea, as it pleased Him, as He would, for whatever He would, He could; looked at what He would; He fixed His eyes, He saw; He turned away His eyes, and did not see; who followed was behind Him, whoso could be seen, before Him; with the eyes of His Body, He saw only what was before Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But from His Divinity nothing was hid.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Put aside, put aside, I say, for a while the form of the servant, look at the Form of God in which He was before the world was made; in which He was equal to the Father; hereby receive and understand what He saith to thee, ‘Who being in the form of God, thought it not robbery to be equal with God.’

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There see Him if thou canst, that thou mayest be able to see what His ‘Seeing’ is.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “In the beginning was the Word.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How doth the Word see?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hath the Word eyes, or are our eyes found in Him, the eyes not of the flesh, but the eyes of godly hearts?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For, “Blessed are the pure in heart, for they shall see God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Christ thou seest Man and God; He doth manifest to thee the Man, God He reserveth for thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now see how He reserveth God for thee, who doth manifest Himself to thee as Man.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Whoso loveth Me,” saith He, “keepeth My commandments; whoso loveth Me shall be loved of My Father, and I will love him.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And as if it were asked, “What wilt Thou give to him whom Thou lovest?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “And I will manifest Myself,” saith He, “to him.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What meaneth this, Brethren?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He whom they saw already, promised that He would manifest Himself to them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Those by whom He was seen, or those also by whom He was not seen?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thus speaking to a certain Apostle, who asked to see the Father, that it might suffice him, and said, “Show us the Father, and it sufficeth us” —Then He standing before this servant’s eyes, in the form of a servant, reserving for his eyes when deified the Form of God, saith to him, “Have I been so long time with you, and have ye not known Me?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He that seeth Me, seeth the Father also.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou askest to see the Father; see Me, thou seest Me, and dost not see Me.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou seest what for thee I have assumed, thou dost not see What I have reserved for thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Give ear to My commandments, purify thine eyes.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “For whoso loveth Me, keepeth My commandments, and I will love him.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To him as keeping My commandments, and by My commandments made whole will I manifest Myself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If then, Brethren, we are not able to see what the “Seeing” of the Word is, whither are we going? what Vision it may be with too great haste are we requiring? why are we wishing to have shown us what we are not able to see?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: These things accordingly are spoken of which we desire to see, not as what we are able already to comprehend.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if thou seest the “Seeing” of the Word, peradventure in that thou seest the “Seeing” of the Word, thou wilt see the Word Himself; that the Word may not be one thing, the “Seeing” of the Word another, lest there be Therein anything joined, and coupled, and double, and compacted.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For It is something Simple, of a Simplicity ineffable.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Not as with a man, the man is one thing, the man’s seeing another.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For sometimes a man’s seeing is extinguished, and the man remains.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This it is of which I said that I was about to say something which all would not be able to understand; the Lord even grant that some may have understood.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: My Brethren, to this end doth He exhort us, that we may see, that the “Seeing” of the Word is beyond our powers; for they are small; be they nourished, perfected.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: By the commandments.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What commandments?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “He that loveth Me, keepeth My commandments.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What commandments?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For already do we wish to increase, to be strengthened, perfected, that we may see the “Seeing” of the Word.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Tell us, Lord, now what commandments?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “A new commandment I give unto you, that ye love one another.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This charity then, Brethren, let us draw from the plentifulness of the Fountain, let us receive it; be nourished by it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Receive thou that whereby thou mayest be able to receive.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let charity give thee birth, let charity nourish thee; charity bring thee to perfection, charity strengthen thee; that thou mayest see this “Seeing” of the Word, that the Word is not one thing and His “Seeing” another, but that the “Seeing” of the Word is the Very Word Himself; and so perhaps thou wilt soon understand that that which is said, “The Son can do nothing of Himself, but what He seeth the Father do,” is as if He had said, “The Son would not be, if He had not been born of the Father.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let this suffice, Brethren; I know that I have said that which perhaps, if meditated upon, may develop itself to many, which oftentimes when expressed in words may chance to be obscured.
